openFindings.title: Redundant tier-downgrade governance_audit emitters across 3 code sites
openFindings.evidence: Three code sites emit action='tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path'. Handler condition (previousTier==='constitutional' && nextTier==='important') can overlap with SQL-layer unconditional emit when a previously-constitutional row's path is non-constitutional AND request is constitutional — produces duplicate audits.

openFindings.title: Wave-1 strategy description drift: importance_tier still in ALLOWED_POST_INSERT_COLUMNS
openFindings.evidence: strategy.md claims the allowlist removal as Wave-1 P1-018 fix; in practice the allowlist still contains 'importance_tier' (post-insert-metadata.ts:56) but the guard was hoisted above the SET builder. Functional equivalence holds; documentation drift only.

openFindings.title: Constitutional→critical downgrade emits no governance_audit
openFindings.evidence: Guard only triggers for importanceTier==='constitutional' requests. A request importanceTier='critical' against a currently-constitutional row on a non-constitutional path downgrades to 'critical' without any typed governance_audit. Handler audit condition nextTier==='important' also fails. Observability gap, not Invariant-3 violation.

evidence: 12-site SQL-write census confirms no bypass candidate
post-insert-metadata.ts:88-128 — guard hoisted inside applyPostInsertMetadata prior to SET builder; functionally equivalent to allowlist removal
